figures.caption: Figure 6: Alpamayo1 open-loop ASR under varying query budgets and success thresholds across reasoning and trajectory objectives.
Table 11: Trajectory and reasoning deviations measured before closed-loop incident onset.
Table 4: Open-loop ASR and downstream safety impact across reasoning and trajectory targets.
Table 14: Closed-loop reasoning ASR sensitivity to temporal consistency k. Semantic deviation uses ϵ = 0.5, while slowdown uses a 25% excess token-length threshold after subtracting benign variation.
Table 1: Open-loop ASR and query efficiency across reasoning and trajectory targets.
Figure 2: Threat model: imperfect language interfaces can expose reasoning-enabled driving models to malformed textual inputs, leading to unsafe behavior.
Table 9: Effect of augmentation strength on open-loop ASR. Larger σ corresponds to stronger textual perturbations.
Table 10: Effect of input token length on open-loop ASR. Longer textual inputs provide a larger perturbable surface and generally increase vulnerability.
Table 7: ASR by malicious prompt semantic intent and prompt type across trajectory, semantic reasoning, and structural objectives.

Figure 8: Closed-loop safety impact of successful trajectory-targeted attacks. Bars compare benign and perturbed rollouts across collision, off-road, and wrong-lane incidents for Alpamayo1 and Alpamayo1.5.

Figure 9: Closed-loop safety impact of successful reasoning-targeted attacks. Bars compare benign and perturbed rollouts across collision, off-road, and wrong-lane incidents for Alpamayo1 and Alpamayo1.5.
Table 5: Effect of clean malicious prompts without textual augmentation. Results compare neutral inputs against malicious prompts with different semantic intents and prompt types at N = 0.
Figure 7: Alpamayo1.5 open-loop ASR under varying query budgets and success thresholds across reasoning and trajectory objectives.
